rules.when: approved tasks are stored outside the runtime queue
rules.rule: surface them in the dashboard until a deterministic approval-to-queue bridge exists

rules.when: dashboard controls are simplified
rules.rule: keep mobile-first cards for status, queue, logs, and pending approvals

rules.when: task registry items span multiple states
rules.rule: group them by status and show a single recommended next action

rules.when: task registry items are used across multiple runs
rules.rule: store stable ids and timestamps so future state transitions and audits are deterministic

rules.when: approval actions hand tasks into execution
rules.rule: approve through the same queue safety checks used by manual submissions and append an audit history entry

rules.when: queue execution changes the real state of an approved registry task
rules.rule: mirror the runtime outcome back into tasks.json with deterministic history entries so approved work does not stay stale after execution

rules.when: task board cards need retry and completion context
rules.rule: normalize execution and history fields in the task-registry API before rendering them in the mobile dashboard

rules.when: approved work is recovered directly from a verified working tree
rules.rule: clear stale queue entries and mark the registry task complete in the same change so the loop does not re-run recovered work

rules.when: manual recovery completes approved work outside the queue
rules.rule: append a deterministic manual_recovery record to tasks.log and regenerate metrics.json from the registry snapshot so observability reflects the recovered outcome

rules.when: dashboard task actions mutate tasks.json directly
rules.rule: regenerate codex-learning/metrics.json in the same request path so approval and rejection changes persist between queue runs

rules.when: the task registry backlog grows beyond a single mobile screen
rules.rule: add lightweight filters or collapse completed states before adding more card detail so operators can triage quickly on iPhone-sized layouts

rules.when: queue or orchestrator shell helpers change while the tmux session is already running
rules.rule: restart the queue session before trusting runtime behavior, because long-lived shells keep the helper definitions they sourced at startup

rules.when: retry counters are written from both shell helpers and embedded Python maintenance paths
rules.rule: use the same hashed retry key everywhere and clear legacy alias files during writes or cleanup so retry state stays deterministic

rules.when: obsolete retry alias files are still present in the runtime retry directory
rules.rule: prune legacy `project__task.retry` aliases during runtime directory setup so observability matches the canonical hashed retry store without waiting for a task rewrite

rules.when: automation memory under `$CODEX_HOME` is unset or outside the writable sandbox
rules.rule: mirror the same concise run summary into `projects/<project>/automation-memory/<automation-id>.md` and record that external sync is pending instead of dropping the memory update

rules.when: automation memory under `$CODEX_HOME` is writable again on a later run
rules.rule: merge any mirrored summaries into the real `$CODEX_HOME/automations/<automation-id>/memory.md` path and write the new summary there before falling back to the workspace mirror on an actual path failure

rules.when: a scheduled automation starts with a missing external memory file but a workspace mirror exists
rules.rule: resolve the external automation home from `CODEX_HOME` or the default `~/.codex`, then hydrate the external memory file from the mirror when possible, or read the mirror directly before picking the next improvement, so prior outcomes influence the current run before any new summary is appended

rules.when: creating or loading metadata for a managed project outside codex-agent-system
rules.rule: require an explicit external workspace path before execution; do not default runtime work into projects/<name> inside Codex Control

rules.when: codex exec hits a persistent authentication failure
rules.rule: cache the auth failure reason briefly, pause queue processing while auth is unavailable, and skip repeated live codex calls until the cooldown expires

rules.when: a cached Codex auth failure is blocking the queue
rules.rule: surface the blocker reason and remaining cooldown directly in dashboard status and next-action messaging before inviting retries or new approvals

rules.when: Codex auth is blocked while operators review pending approvals
rules.rule: reject dashboard approval transitions immediately, but keep pending task edits available so operators can prepare work before auth recovers

rules.when: pending approval task text or project metadata needs correction from mobile
rules.rule: edit the task inline in the dashboard before queue handoff and append an audit history entry for the change

rules.when: the dashboard offers a direct queue action outside the approval backlog
rules.rule: remove or downgrade the action into pending approval until deterministic server-side task shaping exists; do not let vague raw prompts or compatibility endpoints bypass human review

rules.when: strategy-seeded or anomaly-generated tasks have already failed twice with the same intent
rules.rule: treat the failed equivalents as saturated and stop recreating the same title/template until a different bounded experiment is available

rules.when: bounded child retries under the same failed root have already failed twice
rules.rule: stop generating another `bounded_failed_step_child` descendant for that root and fall back to a different strategy family instead of burning more retries on the same decomposition path

rules.when: distinct task-registry records share the same queue text
rules.rule: scope retry state to the selected registry task id and fall back to execution.attempt instead of reusing title-keyed retry files across different task instances

rules.when: a queue lane claims one registry task among several same-title siblings
rules.rule: carry the claimed task id through execution-state syncs so start/completion/failure updates mutate the exact claimed record instead of re-resolving by queue text

rules.when: post-run execution context is persisted after a same-title task was claimed explicitly
rules.rule: thread the claimed task id into run-context persistence as well, so failure_context and execution_context attach to the exact claimed record instead of the most recently updated same-title sibling

rules.when: a same-title lease needs to be released after an explicit claim
rules.rule: release the lease by claimed task id when available, instead of freeing whichever same-title approved/running record happens to match first

rules.when: the strategy loop intentionally stops retrying a repeated failed system task
rules.rule: surface the saturation state in task-registry summaries so operators know to pick a different bounded experiment instead of assuming the loop is stuck

rules.when: strategy saturation is only visible in dashboard task summaries
rules.rule: persist a compact saturation count and boolean in metrics.json so later decision paths can reuse repeated-failure signals without rescanning the full registry

rules.when: auto approval is enabled for strategy seed or anomaly work and an equivalent strategy family has already failed
rules.rule: keep the next strategy task in pending_approval instead of auto-queuing it so observed failures influence the next attempt before retry budget is spent again

rules.when: enterprise readiness seeding has multiple viable templates and some have already failed more often than others
rules.rule: prefer the least-failed template first so task selection reuses failure history instead of re-seeding the same weaker experiment

rules.when: enterprise readiness seed templates are tied on failed-equivalent count
rules.rule: prefer categories with a positive observed win rate in the current project before falling back to static template order, so future enterprise seeds reuse recent outcomes instead of creation order

rules.when: multiple failed-task follow-ups are competing for a limited pending-approval slot
rules.rule: prefer source families from categories with a positive observed project-local win rate before falling back to pure recency, so bounded follow-ups learn from the categories that have already resolved successfully

rules.when: strategy category ranking has no resolved project-local history for a category
rules.rule: fall back to the configured category success prior from priority.json, but keep any positive local observed win rate ahead of that prior so early planning stays deterministic without overriding real project evidence

rules.when: provider histories for a category are tied on learned success rate and task count
rules.rule: prefer the provider with the lower avg total step attempts, so routing reuses the same bounded retry cap while still learning which provider finishes with less aggregate loop effort

rules.when: retry churn or queue starvation is derived from persisted task state
rules.rule: persist the compact board-health booleans through the shared task-metrics path so strategy, sync, and dashboard reuse the same deterministic signal

rules.when: strategy consumes persisted retry-churn health to seed follow-up work
rules.rule: treat the shared retry-churn signal as actionable only when current approved, running, or stale-running work still carries retry pressure; do not seed from historical multi-attempt resolutions alone

rules.when: stale running tasks are reconciled before strategy plans new anomaly work
rules.rule: remove the separate runtime-state mismatch anomaly and let queue-drain or retry-pressure decisions operate on the reconciled registry state instead of duplicating the stale-running diagnosis

rules.when: external web research is used to influence self-improvement
rules.rule: ingest only bounded configured feeds into a deterministic snapshot and route each fresh external signal through pending approval before broader system changes are derived

rules.when: strategy performs external web or media research autonomously
rules.rule: run the research worker inside Docker, keep the source list explicitly configured, and prefer transcript- or subtitle-backed media inputs so the resulting knowledge snapshot stays deterministic and reviewable

rules.when: queue-side low-completion anomaly seeding considers the `queue_drain_completion_guard` buffer task after two equivalent failed runs
rules.rule: treat that buffer experiment as saturated and do not auto-approve another copy until a different bounded follow-up is available

rules.when: strategy planning sees the `system_work_buffer` queue-drain family already fail twice
rules.rule: skip that saturated buffer template and let the next bounded backlog fallback seed instead of recreating the same pending-approval task

rules.when: browser-based dashboard acceptance is used to validate a redesign
rules.rule: assert rendered hierarchy and operator interactions in a live browser session instead of trusting screenshot artifacts alone; verify search and density controls directly in the page

rules.when: bounded external research can influence future planning
rules.rule: persist compact research freshness, error, and latest-source provenance in shared metrics so operators and later decision paths can see whether external signals are current before acting on them

rules.when: strategy seeds a follow-up from an external research signal
rules.rule: reuse the persisted research status/error snapshot to lower task confidence when shared external-signal metrics are stale or erroring, so external follow-ups do not outrank stronger internal evidence

rules.when: dashboard search/highlight behavior watches the board for refresh mutations
rules.rule: never observe subtree mutations while also rewriting descendant innerHTML for highlights; watch only structural list changes or guard the observer so board tooling cannot trigger an unresponsive browser loop

rules.when: pending approval tasks are auto-repaired into approval-ready titles and shapes
rules.rule: preserve strategy-specific context in the repaired title and ignore timestamp-only task_shape diffs so repeated dashboard reads do not rewrite the same task again

rules.when: enterprise-readiness strategy seeds a new pending approval task
rules.rule: persist the same compact task_intent metadata used by other strategy paths so approval shaping, metrics, and later strategy decisions can reuse the seed deterministically

rules.when: bounded failed-step follow-up tasks inherit long file-scoped step text from a failed plan
rules.rule: auto-repair them into compact approval-ready objective titles keyed to the failed strategy signal, so the approval queue does not stall on deterministic child tasks

rules.when: pending approval still contains board-health follow-ups sourced from persisted retry-churn or first-pass strategy families
rules.rule: compact them to the family-level approval title before rendering or persisting the board row, so approval readiness and duplicate detection stay deterministic

rules.when: the resident dashboard backend entrypoint changes after agentctl started the session
rules.rule: treat `codex-dashboard/server.js` drift the same as shell-helper drift and require a reload before trusting approval reshaping or prompt-intake behavior

rules.when: runtime reload drift is marked restart-needed while pending approvals are still open
rules.rule: block approval handoff server-side and surface the restart reason directly in the approval UI so stale dashboard logic cannot enqueue new work

rules.when: a recurring queue-buffer seed family has a newer completed equivalent after older failures
rules.rule: count only failures since the latest success and let the queue-drain buffer recur when backlog pressure returns, instead of keeping the family permanently saturated from stale failures

rules.when: approval handoff is blocked by runtime reload drift
rules.rule: show the exact existing `agentctl.sh reload` workflow in the approval surface so operators can recover without leaving the pending queue guessing at the next step

rules.when: runtime helper fingerprints are computed inside minimal fixtures or partial worktrees
rules.rule: hash missing tracked helper files as a deterministic sentinel instead of failing, so reload-drift detection still works when optional runtime surfaces like the dashboard are absent

rules.when: orchestrator runs span multiple plan steps with per-step retries
rules.rule: persist the bounded max retry count as `attempts` and track aggregate loop effort separately as `total_step_attempts`, so safety limits, provider learning, and failure context do not treat normal multi-step work as excess retries

rules.when: dashboard task details summarize execution attempts after bounded retry accounting is introduced
rules.rule: show aggregate loop effort as `total_step_attempts` only when it exceeds the bounded retry count, so operators can see real multi-step work without mistaking it for a retry-budget violation

rules.when: persisted metrics summarize task pressure after bounded retry accounting is introduced
rules.rule: track loop effort from `total_step_attempts - attempts` at the task level instead of treating bounded retry counts as a proxy for multi-step work, so future analysis can separate retry pressure from normal execution depth

rules.when: persistent loop effort is already detected and multiple failed follow-up families are competing
rules.rule: keep existing learned success signals first, but break the remaining tie toward the lower-effort bounded follow-up before recency so strategy spends the next slot reducing execution drag instead of retrying broader work

rules.when: persistent loop effort is already detected and enterprise seed templates are tied on failed-equivalent and category-success ranking
rules.rule: prefer categories with lower observed extra step attempts before static template order so enterprise backlog growth shifts toward work that has executed with less drag

rules.when: an approved task was marked `already_queued` at approval time but its live queue entry later disappears
rules.rule: treat `already_queued` as a recoverable queue handoff state and enqueue the approved task again when it is no longer present in the queue or running set, so duplicate-safe approval does not strand approved work until a runtime restart
